_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House batch HD54-58 (Buckley, Hickland, Curry, Hayes, Kerwin). State tier. Clears party-default inferred-trues to null; documents only cited positions. Fixes bogus Tennessee-TSRA citations on Curry/Kerwin and Hayes freshman mislabel.

profile.background: Veterinarian (DVM, Texas A&M 1993) and Salado rancher; 4th-term R, HD-54 (Bell Co./Fort Cavazos). Chair, House Public Education Committee (89th). Filed for 2026 reelection.

brad-buckley@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Pro-life: Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 97%; co-authored the Texas Heartbeat Bill and supported SB8; documented YES votes on SB8/HB1280/SB4 (medication-abortion limits). Marriage: iVoterGuide profile records he opposes legal recognition of same-sex unions (SOFT survey). School choice: as Public Education chair authored HB3 (ESA vehicle) and was House sponsor of the enacted SB2 ESA program. Border: iVoterGuide records strong support for border-wall construction (SOFT survey). Guns: NRA-PVF/Texas State Rifle Association 'AQ' rating; pro-2A campaign statements. DEVIATION (fiscal, high-value): Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade 'F' and middling YCT (~67) — driven by his big-dollar public-school-finance bills (HB2 $7.7B) as Public Ed chair, NOT a social-issue deviation. Individual roll calls on SB14/HB1927/SB1/SB4 not retrievable this pass -> left null. No evidence found on euthanasia/IVF, election integrity, public justice, federal overreach -> null (not inferred).

profile.background: Freshman R, HD-55 (central Bell Co.); elected 2024, defeated incumbent Hugh Shine in primary. B.A. Baylor, M.C.J. Boston Univ.; crisis-pregnancy-center mentor. On Homeland Security/Public Safety & Veterans' Affairs and Ways & Means. Running for reelection 2026.

hillary-hickland@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier). Pro-life: Texas Right to Life endorsement (2024 & 2026), Texas Alliance for Life 2025 scorecard 93%; primary author HB1980 (retroactive child support from conception), joint author HB4595 (bars transporting a minor for abortion); Freedom Voter Guide survey: opposes physician-assisted suicide. Sexuality: backed SB8 ('Women's Privacy Act'/bathroom bill) on the floor (cited Scripture vs Rep. Anchia); FVG opposes SOGI protected classes and forced-pronoun use. School choice: voted FOR SB2 (ESA voucher) and to table the put-it-on-the-ballot amendment; primary author HB1434 (narrows affirmative defense for obscene material harmful to children). Christian liberty: cosponsor SB10 (Ten Commandments) and SB11 (school prayer), defended both publicly; FVG supports conscience protections. Guns: Gun Owners of America endorsement (HD-55). No documented evidence on election integrity, public justice (committee seat only), federal overreach, economic-stewardship specifics, same-sex-marriage stance, IVF -> null (not inferred). No cross-party deviation found.

profile.religion: Non-denominational Christian (Renew Church / Christ the King, Waco)
profile.background: R, HD-56 (western McLennan Co./Waco); took office Nov 2024 succeeding retired Charles 'Doc' Anderson. BBA finance Texas A&M; owns PJC Investments LLC (investment holding, NOT homebuilding). On Delivery of Government Efficiency and Transportation committees. Facing a 2026 primary challenge from the anti-Burrows right (Ralph Patterson).

pat-curry@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier) — REPLACES prior all-true party-default archetype and removes a bogus 'Tennessee State Rifle Association PAC' citation that was wrongly footnoted on his 2A answers (he is a Texas legislator). Documented: pro-life (Texas Alliance for Life 2025 100%, authored an 89R life bill, self-states opposes abortion except mother's life); transgender (iVoterGuide: opposes biological males in women's sports/spaces, calls child gender-transition abuse; Texas Values Action 91%); school choice (voted YES on SB2 ESA voucher — corrected from an erroneous union log; Texas Home School Coalition endorsement); Ten Commandments (publicly supports SB10, own quote); photo voter ID (iVoterGuide); police backing (Waco Police Officers Association endorsement); anti-sanctuary-city (iVoterGuide); 2A (self-states '100% Second Amendment', NRA endorsement). FACTION NUANCE (high-value): NOT a Burrows voter — voted David Cook for Speaker; YCT 71% / CPAC 72% (middling); TRTL 82% vs TAL 100% gap; active 2026 anti-establishment primary challenge. No genuine cross-party LEFT deviation. No evidence on euthanasia/IVF, federal overreach, E-Verify/birthright, NFA/castle-doctrine -> null.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (Immaculate Conception Catholic Church, Denton)
profile.background: R, HD-57 (Denton Co.); second term (took office Jan 2023, reelected 2024 — NOT a freshman). Attorney (real-estate law), former Bexar Co. Assistant Criminal DA; Texas House Freedom Caucus member. Won 2026 GOP primary; faces Ray Stith (D) in the Nov 2026 general.

richard-hayes@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier) — replaces all-true party-default archetype and corrects the 'freshman' mislabel (he is second-term, took office Jan 2023). Documented: pro-life (Texas Right to Life 89th 100%, Texas Alliance for Life 93%); school choice (voted YES on SB2 ESA voucher — only R 'no' votes were Phelan and VanDeaver — plus campaign statement); 2A (self-states strong supporter; Life Member of TSRA, NRA, and GOA). YCT 88th rating 98 (2nd-most-conservative House member). Border security is a stated top priority but no specific barrier/military or deportation position -> null. No documented evidence on marriage/sexuality (his individual votes not locatable), Christian liberty (SB10 vote unconfirmed), economic specifics, election integrity, public justice (DA role is biography, not advocacy), or federal overreach -> null (not inferred). No cross-party deviation found.

profile.background: Freshman R, HD-58 (Johnson/Somervell Cos.); elected 2024, beat incumbent DeWayne Burns in primary runoff. Longtime Glen Rose innkeeper, former Glen Rose mayor; mother of U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins. On Culture/Recreation/Tourism and Public Education committees. Won 2026 primary vs Mary Wells; faces Chris Oldham (D) in general.

helen-kerwin@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 (state tier) — REPLACES all-true party-default archetype and removes a bogus 'Tennessee State Rifle Association PAC' citation wrongly footnoted on her 2A answers. Documented: pro-life (Texas Alliance for Life 2025 93% + 2026 endorsement, supported HB7 abortion-drug enforcement, iVoterGuide abortion-only-for-mother's-life); marriage/sex (iVoterGuide affirms marriage as God-ordained union, opposes govt-funded gender transition; supported HB229 defining sex as biological); school choice (voted YES on SB2 ESA voucher — her signature issue, beat Burns over it — supported HB2674 homeschool deregulation; HB1151 parental medical-decision protection); Christian liberty (supported SB11 school prayer; iVoterGuide opposes forcing businesses against religious belief); anti-ESG for public funds (iVoterGuide); police backing (Texas Municipal Police Association + CLEAT endorsements); border (iVoterGuide strongly supports physically securing the southern border). DEVIATION FLAG (guns): she answered 'current laws are sufficient' — softer than constitutional-carry expansion — and no NRA/TSRA grade exists, so only the broad gun-control-opposition cell is scored, not the prior 5 straight trues. No evidence on euthanasia/IVF, election integrity specifics, federal overreach -> null.
